verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone, but there is extensive, well-documented surface display on tumor cells (cancer cell-surface GRP78) under ER stress and oncogenic transformation. Multiple clinical and preclinical programs target cell-surface GRP78 with antibodies, peptide-drug conjugates, and CAR-T approaches. This is the prototypical example of cell-state-induced ecto-display of a nominally intracellular chaperone. Not yes because baseline localization is ER-luminal with no native PM anchor; surface pool requires stress/transformation context.
